arts.ttl: Chelsea have signed ‘next Lamine Yamal’ ahead of next season
arts.sum: Chelsea have won the race to sign highly-rated Sporting CP youngster Geovany Quenda, beating Manchester United to his signature in a deal that will see him arrive at Stamford Bridge in 2026. The winger had been Man United’s top target, with the Premier League giants eager to bring him to Old Trafford. Man United rival […] The post Chelsea have signed ‘next Lamine Yamal’ ahead of next season appeared first on CaughtOffside.

arts.ttl: Barcelona star spotted back in training before Girona
arts.sum: A leading member of the attacking ranks at La Liga giants Barcelona has returned to training alongside the rest of the club’s first-team squad. The player in question? Raphinha. Wide-man…

arts.ttl: Flick discusses Gavi’s best position, reveals why he loves ‘great kid’ Fermín López
arts.sum: Two players who did step up in their absence in the midweek win over Osasuna were Gavi and Fermín López, who were excellent in all aspects of the game and will likely play a big part over the next few weeks as Barça make a push in all competitions. Hansi Flick was asked about both players in a press conference on Saturday, and he started by discussing what Gavi’s best position is and how much he impact the game with his ability to press and keep possession.

arts.ttl: Jonathan David latest: Barcelona 'in competition' with six Premier League clubs for 107-goal sharpshooter
arts.sum: Barcelona are allegedly facing competition from six Premier League clubs for Lille striker Jonathan David, including Manchester United, Chelsea and Liverpool.

arts.ttl: Chelsea Transfer News: Blues sign 'the new Lamine Yamal' in Geovany Quenda
arts.sum: Chelsea will have their own Lamine Yamal when Geovany Quenda joins in 2026, according to one of his former coaches. Chelsea sign 'the new Lamine Yamal'

arts.ttl: Flick wants Barça to embrace schedule challenges
arts.sum: Barcelona are ready for the challenges of a packed schedule of matches as they bid for silverware on three fronts, Hansi Flick said on Saturday.

arts.ttl: Flick reveals why Yamal doesn’t want to rest, admits Ansu Fati ‘not in an easy situation’ at Barça
arts.sum: As for Fati, who continues to sit on the bench and not be given any minutes as his future at the club looks more and more bleak, Flick says it’s simply a matter of other players being ahead of the young forward in the depth chart, but admitted it’s not an easy situation for Ansu to be in given his effort in training. As Barcelona approach the business end of the season, two young forwards find themselves in completely different ends of the spectrum when it comes to game time: Lamine Yamal plays every single minute of every game, and Ansu Fati can’t play at all.

arts.ttl: Barcelona gave up Trent Alexander-Arnold move for 'two reasons' with Liverpool star set to join Real Madrid
arts.sum: Barcelona reportedly gave up their pursuit of Trent Alexander-Arnold due to two key reasons, with the Liverpool star set to join Real Madrid this summer.

arts.ttl: One in, one out: Barcelona ready potential €60m transfer that could impact Man United & Chelsea
arts.sum: Barcelona are reportedly lining up a potential summer transfer window move for Bournemouth centre-back Dean Huijsen. The talented young Spain international has shone for the Cherries this season, and it seems only a matter of time before he earns himself a big move. arts.ttl: Hansi Flick: 'I enjoy being at Barça a lot'
arts.sum: Hansi Flick appeared excited at the challenges facing him and his squad in this final phase of the season when he appeared before the press on Saturday at the Ciutat Esportiva ahead of the game at the Estadi Olímpic. Of the 10 league games remaining, six will be played at the Estadi Olímpic, starting with Barça v Girona.
